परमात्मने.name: परमात्मने
परमात्मने.summary: The Supreme Soul or Universal Spirit, the ultimate reality that pervades everything.

विश्वमाङ्गल्यमूर्तये.name: विश्वमाङ्गल्यमूर्तये
विश्वमाङ्गल्यमूर्तये.summary: The embodiment of universal well-being and auspiciousness for the entire world.

प्रकृतिः.name: प्रकृतिः
प्रकृतिः.summary: The fundamental creative power or Nature, through which the universe is manifested.

पञ्चभूतानि.name: पञ्चभूतानि
पञ्चभूतानि.summary: The five great elements: Prithvi (Earth), Apas (Water), Agni (Fire), Vayu (Air), and Akasha (Ether).

हिमालय.name: हिमालय
हिमालय.summary: The 'King of Mountains', forming the northern crown of Bharat and the abode of gods.

भारतमातरम्.name: भारतमातरम्
भारतमातरम्.summary: The personification of the Indian subcontinent as a divine Mother Goddess.

महेन्द्र.name: महेन्द्र
महेन्द्र.summary: A sacred mountain range in the East (Odisha), associated with Sage Parshurama.

मलय.name: मलय
मलय.summary: The mountain range in the South known for its fragrant sandalwood forests.

सह्य.name: सह्य
सह्य.summary: The Western Ghats, a vast mountain range parallel to the western coast of India.

रैवतक.name: रैवतक
रैवतक.summary: Mount Girnar in Gujarat, sacred to Hindus and Jains, where Lord Krishna held many pastimes.

विन्ध्य.name: विन्ध्य
विन्ध्य.summary: The central mountain range that traditionally divides North and South India.

आरावलि.name: आरावलि
आरावलि.summary: One of the world's oldest fold mountain systems, located in Western India.

गङ्गा.name: गङ्गा
गङ्गा.summary: The holiest river of Bharat, descending from the Himalayas to purify the land.

सरस्वती.name: सरस्वती
सरस्वती.summary: The mythical and sacred river of knowledge and wisdom, now lost to the sands of time.

सिन्धु.name: सिन्धु
सिन्धु.summary: The Indus River, which gave India its name and was the site of its earliest civilization.

ब्रह्मपुत्र.name: ब्रह्मपुत्र
ब्रह्मपुत्र.summary: The 'Son of Brahma', one of the largest rivers in Asia, flowing through Tibet, India, and Bangladesh.

गण्डकी.name: गण्डकी
गण्डकी.summary: A sacred river in Nepal and India, known for producing the Shaligram stones.

कावेरी.name: कावेरी
कावेरी.summary: The 'Ganga of the South', a lifeline for the people of Karnataka and Tamil Nadu.

यमुना.name: यमुना
यमुना.summary: The sacred river of devotion, flowing past Mathura and Vrindavan, associated with Lord Krishna.

रेवा.name: रेवा
रेवा.summary: Another name for the Narmada River, which flows through the heart of India.

कृष्णा.name: कृष्णा
कृष्णा.summary: One of the major rivers of South India, originating in the Sahyadri mountains.

गोदा.name: गोदा
गोदा.summary: The Godavari River, often called the 'Gautami' or 'Vriddha Ganga' in South India.

महानदी.name: महानदी
महानदी.summary: A major river in East Central India, flowing through Chhattisgarh and Odisha.

रत्नाकर.name: रत्नाकर
रत्नाकर.summary: The Indian Ocean, which surrounds the Indian peninsula on three sides.

ग्रहा.name: ग्रहा
ग्रहा.summary: The nine celestial influencers (Navagrahas) in Indian astrology and cosmology.

लोकाः.name: लोकाः
लोकाः.summary: The fourteen planes of existence (worlds) mentioned in the Puranas.

स्वराः.name: स्वराः
स्वराः.summary: The seven primary notes (Saptaswara) of Indian classical music: Sa Re Ga Ma Pa Dha Ni.

दिशः.name: दिशः
दिशः.summary: The ten directions, including the eight cardinal points and Zenit/Nadir.

कालः.name: कालः
कालः.summary: Time, the eternal witness and the foundation of the Indian calendar (Panchang).
